segments.text: We are going to solve this algebraic limit in detail, and we start by evaluating this function when x takes the value 0.
Let's see, in the numerator x takes the value 0, then 1 plus 0 gives us 1.
Here we have 2 times 0 would be 0, and 1 plus 0 gives us 1.
Here 3 times 0 would be 0, 1 plus 0 gives us 1, and all this minus 1.
And in the denominator x also changes by 0.
We solve up here, 1 times 1 times 1 gives us 1, we will have 1 minus 1, and all this over 0.
We solve in the numerator 1 minus 1 gives us 0, and we get to 0 over 0, which is an undetermined form.
It is an indicator that we must do something to that expression.
The strategy that we are going to use is to solve all this that we have in the numerator,
and see what else could be done to this algebraic fraction.
Here we have the product of three binomials.
Let's remember the following, when three quantities are multiplied,
we have the option to group the first two to multiply by the third,
or we can also group the last two to be the first product,
and what gives us to multiply it by the first quantity.
It is what is called the associative property of multiplication.
Well, there we are going to do that, we are going to use this option.
We are going to group the first two binomials that are multiplying.
Then we will have a limit when x tends to 0 of the following.
We open a corchete to make that association or grouping,
and there we will have 1 plus x, which multiplies with 1 plus 2x.
There we have then grouped the first two factors,
and that is multiplied by the third factor, 1 plus 3x.
All that is minus 1, and in the denominator remains x.
Now we are going to solve this product of binomials that we group with corchetes.
There we are going to apply the distributive property,
multiplying the terms, all with all.
So let's see, we have a limit when x tends to 0 of the following.
We are going to open a corchete and we will have 1 by 1 is 1,
1 times plus 2x gives us plus 2x, then plus x times plus 1 is plus x,
and plus x times plus 2x gives us plus 2x squared.
We close the corchete and we write the other factor that remains there in wait,
and all that is minus 1, and in the denominator remains x.
Now we are going to reduce similar terms in this expression that we obtained.
These two terms are the only ones that meet that condition, because they contain x.
So let's see, we have a limit when x tends to 0 of the following.
We will have 1, 2x plus x is plus 3x, and that is added with 2x squared.
This we can already protect with parentheses, that in turn is multiplying with 1 plus 3x,
and all that is minus 1, while in the denominator remains x.
Now we are going to solve this product that occurs between a trinomial and a binomial.
We will have then limit when x tends to 0,
so let's see how we have that product.
We are going to start distributing this 1,
then 1 multiplies to each of these two terms,
1 times 1 gives us 1, and 1 times plus 3x is plus 3x.
Now we are going to distribute what the term 3x is,
3x multiplies to each of these terms,
then we will have 3x times 1 is plus 3x,
3x times plus 3x gives us plus 9x squared.
And now we are going to distribute the other term, which is 2x squared,
we are going to multiply these two terms,
we will have 2x squared times 1 is plus 2x squared,
2x squared times plus 3x would be plus 6x cubed,
and all that is minus 1, while in the denominator remains x.
Now, in all this polynomial that we obtained,
we are going to make the reduction of similar terms.
We have, for example, plus 1 and minus 1,
they are opposite numbers, whose sum gives us 0,
so we can cancel them or eliminate them.
That is what we will have then, limit,
when x tends to 0 of the following,
we review the expression, we can start with the higher degree,
which would be 6x cubed,
now we review those that have a degree of 2,
would be those two terms,
they can be added, they are similar terms,
that gives us plus 11x squared,
and then the terms that contain x,
would be these two,
then plus 3x and plus 3x gives us plus 6x,
and in the denominator remains x.
Now, in the numerator we could extract a common factor,
it is x,
because it is the letter that is repeated in all its terms,
and the minor exponent is extracted,
that is, x to the 1,
then we would have limit,
when x tends to 0 of x,
which comes out as a common factor,
and within the parenthesis we have,
6x squared plus 11x plus 6,
and in the denominator remains x.
Now, there we have an algebraic fraction,
where in the numerator there are two factors,
and there is one that is repeated up and down,
which is x,
then we can cancel or eliminate that factor,
which by the way is the problem factor in this exercise,
as x tends to 0,
then by replacing there,
that is what is causing the indetermination 0 over 0,
that we had obtained at the beginning,
then this is finally left as the limit,
when x tends to 0 of this expression,
6x squared plus 11x plus 6,
and we protect all this using parenthesis.
Finally, we return to evaluate this limit,
we will replace x when it takes the value 0,
in this expression,
then we would have 6 times 0 squared plus 11 times 0 plus 6,
these first two terms become 0,
and then in the end we have 6 as a result,
that will be the answer for this algebraic limit.
We could check this solution by entering
all this expression to a scientific calculator,
and assigning to x values very close to 0,
both on the left and on the right.
By doing this exercise,
we will see how the values of this expression
are approximated to 6.
